subject: math
source_title: New General Mathematics SSS 1 by M.F. Macrae et al
learning_objectives: Expand numbers in any base using powers of that base
Convert numbers from any base to base ten (denary scale)
Convert numbers from base ten to any other base
Convert decimal fractions (bicimals) in base 2 to base 10
Convert numbers from one base to another base via base ten
Determine unknown base numbers using simultaneous equations

sections.heading: Number Base Systems
sections.content: The most commonly used number system is base ten (denary), using digits 0-9, where each digit's place value is a power of 10. For example, 395 in base 10 = 3 Г— 10ВІ + 9 Г— 10В№ + 5 Г— 10вЃ° = 300 + 90 + 5. Other bases follow the same principle: 645в‚‡ = 6 Г— 7ВІ + 4 Г— 7В№ + 5 Г— 7вЃ°, and 101101в‚‚ = 1 Г— 2вЃµ + 0 Г— 2вЃґ + 1 Г— 2Ві + 1 Г— 2ВІ + 0 Г— 2В№ + 1 Г— 2вЃ°.

sections.heading: Conversion to Base Ten (Denary Scale)
sections.content: To convert a number from any base to base ten, expand it using powers of its base and sum the results. For example: 27в‚€ = 2 Г— 8 + 7 Г— 1 = 16 + 7 = 23. And 11011в‚‚ = 16 + 8 + 0 + 2 + 1 = 27.

sections.heading: Conversion from Base Ten to Other Bases
sections.content: To convert from base ten to another base: (1) divide the number by the new base, (2) record the remainder, (3) keep dividing the quotient until it reaches zero, (4) read remainders from bottom to top. Example: 68в‚Ѓв‚Ђ to base 5 вЂ” 68 Г· 5 = 13 R 3; 13 Г· 5 = 2 R 3; 2 Г· 5 = 0 R 2 вЂ” result: 233в‚….

sections.heading: Bicimals вЂ” Base Two Decimal Fractions
sections.content: Bicimals are base-two fractions using negative powers of 2. To convert a bicimal to a decimal, expand each digit as a power of 2 and sum. For example: 1.101в‚‚ = 1 Г— 2вЃ° + 1 Г— 2вЃ»В№ + 0 Г— 2вЃ»ВІ + 1 Г— 2вЃ»Ві = 1 + 0.5 + 0 + 0.125 = 1.625в‚Ѓв‚Ђ. And 10.011в‚‚ = 2 + 0 + 0.25 + 0.125 = 2.375в‚Ѓв‚Ђ.

sections.heading: Conversion Between Non-Decimal Bases (via Base Ten)
sections.content: To convert from base A to base B, first convert from base A to base 10, then convert from base 10 to base B. Example: 1534в‚† to base 8 вЂ” first, 1534в‚† = 1Г—216 + 5Г—36 + 3Г—6 + 4 = 418в‚Ѓв‚Ђ; then 418в‚Ѓв‚Ђ = 642в‚€.

sections.heading: Finding Unknown Bases from Equations
sections.content: When base values are unknown, convert expressions to base 10 to form simultaneous equations and solve. Example: given 32в‚“ вЂ“ 12бµ§ = 9 and 23в‚“ вЂ“ 21бµ§ = 4, converting gives 3x вЂ“ y = 9 and x вЂ“ y = 1. Subtracting: 2x = 8, so x = 4 and y = 3.
